300	    - Patient: Aerion's men-at-arms (Wate, Yorkel)
301	    - Location: Merchants' row
302	    - Outcome: Dunk freed; identity reveal
303	
304	16. **Dunk is imprisoned** — Lord Ashford's soldiers scoop up Dunk and the others; Dunk is confined to a tower cell.
305	    - Agent: Lord Ashford (his soldiers)
306	    - Patient: Dunk
307	    - Location: Ashford Castle tower cell
308	    - Outcome: Dunk held overnight and through day two
309	
310	17. **Baelor offers Dunk the choice of trial by combat** — In the solar, Baelor explains Dunk faces loss of a hand and a foot, and reminds him he may demand trial by combat.
311	    - Agent: Baelor
312	    - Patient: Dunk
313	    - Location: The solar
314	    - Outcome: Dunk's fate hinges on combat
315	
316	18. **Aerion demands a trial of seven** — At the judgment, Aerion invokes his right to a trial of seven so both he and Daeron may face Dunk; seven champions must fight on each side.
317	    - Agent: Aerion
318	    - Instigator: (Aerion's claim, seconded over Maekar's objection)
319	    - Location: Ashford Castle hall
320	    - Outcome: Dunk must find six more knights
321	
322	19. **Dunk recruits his champions; Steffon betrays him** — Ser Steffon pledges aid then reveals he fights for the accusers ("I am the seventh... but for the other side"); Raymun, Lyonel Baratheon, the two Humfreys, Robyn Rhysling, and (secretly) Baelor take Dunk's side; Raymun is knighted by Lyonel and adopts a green apple.
323	    - Agent: Dunk (and Egg, who recruits Lyonel and Rhysling)
324	    - Patient: Various knights
325	    - Location: The Fossoway pavilion / paddock
326	    - Outcome: Six champions, then Baelor as the seventh; Steffon joins Aerion
327	
328	20. **Baelor secretly joins Dunk's side** — When no knight answers Dunk's plea, a black knight in Valarr's armor rides out; he lifts his visor to reveal Prince Baelor.
329	    - Agent: Baelor
330	    - Patient: Dunk (champion of)
331	    - Instrument: Valarr's borrowed armor
332	    - Location: The lists
333	    - Outcome: Dunk's seventh found; "This man protected the weak, as every true knight must"
334	
335	21. **The Trial of Seven** — Seven defenders against seven accusers charge with tourney lances (Baelor's stratagem) versus war lances. Donnel of Duskendale kills Humfrey Beesbury; Aerion drives a lance through Dunk's side; Baelor unhorses Kingsguard and fights Maekar; Dunk is unhorsed twice and head-struck by Aerion's morningstar.
336	    - Agent: The seven defenders and seven accusers
337	    - Patient: Each other
338	    - Instrument: Tourney lances, war lances, morningstar, mace, swords
339	    - Location: Ashford Meadow (muddy field)
340	    - Outcome: See sub-events below
341	
342	22. **Dunk defeats Aerion and forces his recantation** — Dunk rolls free, drags Aerion down, batters his helm with a shield, wrenches up his visor, and makes him yield; later drags him before Lord Ashford to withdraw the accusation.
343	    - Agent: Dunk
344	    - Patient: Aerion
345	    - Instrument: Aerion's own oak-and-iron shield
346	    - Location: The trial field
347	    - Outcome: "I withdraw my accusation"; trial won for Dunk
348	
349	23. **Maekar's mace kills Baelor** — Fighting his brother, Maekar lands a mace blow to the back of Baelor's borrowed helm; the blow crushes Baelor's skull. He stands, speaks, then collapses when the helm is removed.
350	    - Agent: Maekar
351	    - Patient: Baelor
352	    - Instrument: Spiked mace
353	    - Location: The trial field
354	    - Witness: Dunk, Raymun, Steely Pate
355	    - Outcome: Baelor's death (delayed; he dies as the helm comes off)
356	
357	24. **Baelor's funeral** — As Targaryens, Baelor is given to the fire in Ashford Castle's yard, in a black velvet tunic with a scarlet dragon and a golden open-visored helm.
358	    - Agent: House Targaryen (Daeron II's wishes prevail)
359	    - Patient: Baelor's body
360	    - Location: Ashford Castle yard
361	    - Outcome: Cremation
362	
363	25. **Valarr dismisses Dunk** — Standing vigil, Valarr asks "Why would the gods take him, and leave you?" and bids Dunk begone.
364	    - Agent: Valarr
365	    - Patient: Dunk
366	    - Location: Ashford Castle
367	    - Outcome: Dunk leaves wordless
368	
369	26. **Maekar banishes Aerion and offers Egg to Dunk** — Maekar sends Aerion to Lys, then offers Dunk a place in his household at Summerhall with Egg as squire; Dunk accepts Egg but insists on taking him on the road instead.
370	    - Agent: Maekar
371	    - Patient: Aerion (banished); Egg (given as squire)
372	    - Location: The green pool camp
373	    - Outcome: Maekar walks off silent, then sends Egg the next morning
374	
375	27. **Dunk and Egg set out for Dorne** — Egg arrives at sunrise to serve Dunk; they prepare to ride over the Red Mountains.
376	    - Agent: Dunk and Egg
377	    - Location: The green pool camp
378	    - Outcome: The pair depart together
379	
380	## Spatial Layout & Movement
381	| Phase | Who | Position / Movement | Relative To | Notes |
382	|-------|-----|---------------------|-------------|-------|
383	| Departure | Dunk | Rides from the burial hill toward Ashford, leading three horses | The low hill → the road | Stops at the inn en route |
384	| Arrival | Dunk | Reins up at the edge of Ashford Meadow; turns away to make camp in the trees | Edge of the meadow → the green pool | Half a mile from town and castle |
385	| Assembly | The five champions | Raise pavilions at the north end of the lists, river behind them | North end | Two orange (Ashford sons), one green (Tyrell), one red-and-white (Hardyng), one black (Valarr) |
386	| Confrontation | Challengers vs champions (day one) | Challengers at the south end, champions at the north; they salute the viewing stand, then strike shields and charge | North vs south of the lists | Five north–south lanes; viewing stand on the east |
387	| Concealment/Departure | Tanselle and the puppeteers | Flee west toward Dorne overnight | Merchants' wagons (western verge) → gone | "Well gone is well forgot" |
388	| Assembly | Dunk's seven and the accusers | Defenders gather in the south paddock; accusers ride out of the river mist at the north end | South vs north | Mist and fog over the muddy field |
389	| Confrontation | Trial of seven combatants | Charge across an open field with no barriers; horses slam together; melee scatters across the field | North vs south, then mêlée | Eighty yards between the lines at the start |
390	| Pursuit | Dunk | Drags Aerion across the field to the viewing stand to force his recantation | The field → before Lord Ashford | "By the time he reached the viewing stand... the Bright Prince was brown as a privy" |
391	| Dispersal | Baelor's body | Borne to the funeral pyre in the castle yard | The field → Ashford Castle yard | Targaryen cremation |
392	| Departure | Dunk and Egg | Set out from the green pool camp for Dorne over the Red Mountains | The camp → the road south | Closing scene |
393	
394	## Information Revealed
395	| Information | How Revealed | Known To (Characters) | Known To (Reader Only?) |
396	|-------------|-------------|----------------------|------------------------|
397	| The bald stableboy "Egg" is Prince Aegon Targaryen, Maekar's youngest son | Egg commands Aerion's men and calls Aerion "brother"; later confirmed in the cell | Egg, Aerion, the men-at-arms, then Dunk, Baelor, Maekar | Revealed to reader within the unit |
398	| The drunk lordling at the inn is Prince Daeron, Maekar's heir ("Daeron the Drunken") | He appears at the trial and the camp; named by Raymun and the heralds | The princes, then Dunk | Revealed within the unit |
399	| The black knight in Valarr's armor is Prince Baelor in disguise | Baelor lifts his visor on the field | All present | Revealed within the unit |
400	| "Dunk" chooses the name Ser Duncan the Tall; he is a Flea Bottom orphan with no house | Dunk's own invention and internal recollection | Dunk (and gradually others) | Reader knows from the start |
401	| Egg's brother Daeron stole away from their escort and shaved Egg's head to hide their dragon-bright hair | Egg confesses in the cell | Egg, Daeron, then Dunk, Baelor | Revealed within the unit |
402	| Aerion's childhood cruelty (knife between Egg's legs, threats to make him a "sister," drowning Egg's cat) | Egg tells Dunk in the Fossoway pavilion | Egg, then Dunk, Raymun | Revealed within the unit |
403	| Daeron's lie: he told Maekar a "robber knight" abducted Aegon, casting Dunk as the villain | Baelor explains in the solar; Daeron admits it | Daeron, Maekar, Baelor, then Dunk | Revealed within the unit |
404	| Daeron has true (prophetic) dreams; he dreamed of Dunk and a dead dragon | Daeron tells Dunk at the inn and again on the walk | Daeron, then Dunk | Revealed within the unit |
405	| Maekar's mace dealt Baelor's fatal blow | Maekar admits it to Dunk at the pool | Maekar, Dunk, witnesses | Revealed within the unit |
406	| Aerion withdraws his accusation; Daeron means to withdraw his and not fight | Aerion forced to recant; Daeron tells Dunk privately | All present / Dunk | Revealed within the unit |
407	| Ser Steffon will fight for the accusers, not Dunk | Steffon announces it while arming | Steffon, then Dunk, Raymun | Revealed within the unit |
408	| Maekar sends Aerion to Lys | Maekar tells Dunk at the pool | Maekar, Dunk | Revealed within the unit |
409	| Heraldry of the assembled houses | Narration as Dunk reads the banners | Dunk (taught by Ser Arlan) | Reader informed via Dunk |
410	| The last dragon was a stunted green female whose eggs never hatched; some say Aegon the Unlucky poisoned her | Ser Arlan's recalled tale | Dunk (via Arlan) | Recalled to reader |
411	
412	## Dialogue of Note
413	| Speaker | Listener | Quote/Paraphrase | Context |
414	|---------|----------|------------------|---------|
415	| Daeron | Dunk | "I dreamed of you." / "You stay away from me, do you hear? You stay well away." | The drunk lordling at the inn |
416	| The innkeep | Dunk | "Never you mind that one, ser. All he does is drink and talk about his dreams." | Of Daeron |
417	| Egg | Dunk | "Every knight needs a squire... You look as though you need one more than most." | Egg pressing to be taken on |
418	| Dunk | Egg | "It's all the pavilion a true knight needs. I would sooner sleep under the stars than in some smoky tent." | Of the elm |
419	| Egg | Dunk | "Egg." | Giving his name |
420	| Aerion | Dunk | "Boy, let go of that nag and see to my horse." | Mistaking Dunk for a stableboy |
421	| Aerion | Dunk | "Knighthood has fallen on sad days." | Dismissing Dunk |
422	| Egg | Aerion | "I cut it off, brother. I didn't want to look like you." | The identity reveal at the puppet show |
423	| Aerion | Dunk | "The dragon ought never lose." | Justifying his cruelty to Tanselle |
424	| Daeron | Dunk | "I dreamed of you and a dead dragon... It had fallen on top of you, but you were alive and the dragon was dead." | On the walk in the rain |
425	| Daeron | Dunk | "Do me a kindness if you would, and make certain it is my brother Aerion you slay." | Same walk |
426	| Aerion | Baelor / the court | "A trial of seven... That is my right, I do believe." | Demanding the trial |
427	| Maekar | Dunk | "If a cause is just, good men will fight for it. If you can find no champions, ser, it will be because you are guilty." | At the judgment |
428	| Ser Steffon | Dunk | "I am the seventh... but for the other side. I fight with Prince Aerion and the accusers." | The betrayal |
429	| Raymun | Steffon | "You swore to be a knight." | Renouncing his cousin |
430	| Baelor | The court | "I will take Ser Duncan's side." / "This man protected the weak, as every true knight must." | Joining the trial |
431	| Dunk | The viewing knights | "ARE THERE NO TRUE KNIGHTS AMONG YOU?" | His despairing plea |
432	| Aerion | The field | "The dragon is not mocked." | Mocking Dunk's failed plea |
433	| Steely Pate | Dunk | "A knight who remembered his vows." | Why the smallfolk cheer Dunk |
434	| Dunk / the rhyme | — | "Oak and iron, guard me well... Or else I'm dead, and doomed to hell." | The shield rhyme, taught by Arlan |
435	| Aerion | Dunk | "I yield." | Defeated in the melee |
436	| Valarr | Dunk | "Why would the gods take him, and leave you?" | At Baelor's bier |
437	| Maekar | Dunk | "The realm has as many hedge knights as hedges, and all of them have feet." | Rejecting Dunk's answer about his foot |
438	| Dunk | Maekar | "Daeron never slept in a ditch, I'll wager, and all the beef that Aerion ever ate was thick and rare and bloody, like as not." | Insisting on taking Egg on the road |
439	| Egg | Dunk | "My lord father says I am to serve you." / "I hear they have good puppet shows." | Arriving as squire; closing line |
440	
441	## POV Character's Internal State
442	- **Emotional state:** Grief and loneliness at Ser Arlan's death; awe and unease among lords and princes; mounting dread before the trial; shame at being "Dunk the lunk, thick as a castle wall"; relief and guilt when champions arrive; horror and bewilderment at Baelor's death; weary, hollow grief in the aftermath.
443	- **Primary preoccupation:** Becoming a true knight and winning at least one victory at Ashford ("One victory is all I need"); then survival at the trial; then the meaning of Baelor's death in exchange for his own life.
444	- **Key decisions made:** To ride to Ashford as a knight and adopt the name Ser Duncan the Tall; to take Egg as his squire; to sell Sweetfoot for armor; to intervene against Aerion to protect Tanselle; to demand trial by combat; to accept Egg from Maekar but only on the road, not at Summerhall; to ride for Dorne.
445	- **Self-deception flags:** Reassures himself the stableboy "has a good life here... taking him would be no kindness"; tells himself "This is what it means to be a knight" over a good meal; claims "the luck is mine alone" at the falling star; insists "what was true for [Arlan] need not be true for me" about jousting; repeatedly styles himself a knight while privately doubting ("Dunk of Flea Bottom? Or Ser Duncan the Tall?").

518	| What does Daeron's dream of Dunk and a dead dragon mean? | Daeron / Dunk | "I dreamed of you and a dead dragon... you were alive and the dragon was dead" |

# THK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** THK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (tourney grounds, south bank of the Cockleswent)
- **location_secondary:** A roadside inn; the green pool campsite beneath the elm; Ashford Castle; Ashford town; a low hill (Ser Arlan's grave); the road from the burial site to Ashford
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; covers the ride to Ashford, two days of jousting, the night of recruitment, the trial of seven, and several days of aftermath (Baelor's funeral, Maekar's offer, Egg's arrival). Spans roughly a week within this unit.

| Valyria ("lost Valyria") | Origin of the Targaryens, across the seas | Yes (mentioned) |
| The narrow sea | Crossed by the Andals and the seven gods | Yes (mentioned) |

## Location Descriptions
| Location | Defensive Features | Architecture/Layout | Interior Details | Scale | Condition | Surrounding Terrain/Geography | Notable Sensory Details |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------|------|-----------|------------------------------|------------------------|

| Ashford Meadow | None (a commons) | Three score+ pavilions of sailcloth, linen, and silk in many shapes and colors; merchants' stalls along the field edge; five north–south jousting lanes with waist-high whitewashed barriers; a three-tiered viewing stand on the east with an orange canopy and four high-backed chairs; a quintain on the eastern verge | "A second town... of silk instead of stone" | Larger and fairer than Ashford town | Transformed for the tourney; later a "great morass of grey-brown mud and torn grass" | Serves as a commons for Ashford town; bounded by the river and woods | Banners "brighter than a field of wildflowers"; torchlight at night; fog and pale snakes of mist on the trial morning |
| The green pool campsite | None | A deep pool formed by a bend in a brook; thick reeds; a tall leafy elm "presiding over all"; soft green spring grass | Open camp with a fire ring of rocks | Half a mile from town and castle, on the outskirts of the meadow | Pretty, unclaimed | Birch and ash on the western verge nearby (merchants' wagons) | A dragonfly over the water; "greener even than the banners of the Tyrells and the Estermonts" |
| The roadside inn | None | A tall daub-and-timber building beside a stream; common room, kitchens, stairs, stables | Common room nearly empty; tables; straw mattresses upstairs | — | Functioning | Beside a stream on the road a day's ride south of Ashford | Warm yellow light from the windows; smell of roasted lamb and duck |
| Ashford town | None noted | Whitewashed houses with thatched roofs; Henly's stable | — | A market town, "prettier than most" | Functioning | Across the shallow ford from the meadow, north bank | "Inviting aspect" |

## Artifacts & Objects of Significance

| Ser Arlan's shield (winged chalice → elm and star) | Oaken shield with a scarred metal rim; sigil a winged chalice, silver on brown; rim reforged by Pate (twice as thick, banded); repainted by Tanselle as a green elm on a sunset field with a shooting star | Yes | Dunk (cast aside, then recovered) |
| Chain-mail hauberk (Ser Arlan's) | Scoured of rust a thousand times; too small for Dunk; offered in trade to Pate | Yes | Egg later wears it; offered to Pate |
| Iron halfhelm | Broad nasal, dent on the left temple | Yes | Dunk / camp |

| Maekar's spiked mace | "As deadly-looking a weapon as any Dunk had ever seen"; deals Baelor's fatal blow | Yes | Maekar |
| The dragon puppet (and Florian, Jonquil, Nymeria's ships) | Carved by Tanselle's uncle, painted by Tanselle; the dragon "slain" each show with red-sawdust blood; smashed and burned by Aerion's men | Yes | Destroyed at the meadow |
| Thunder | Dunk's warhorse; big brown stallion, bright eye, fierce spirit; "ridden only in tourney and battle"; carries Dunk in the trial; "veteran of half a hundred fights" | Yes | Dunk |
| Chestnut | Swaybacked old stot; too old to be worth much | Yes | Dunk (to become Egg's mount) |
| Sweetfoot | Ser Arlan's sorrel palfrey; easy gait; sold to Henly for 750 silver stags | Yes | Henly (Ashford town) |
| Valarr's black armor | Borrowed by Baelor for the trial; black plate, lance, shield, trappings, red-dragon crest | Yes | Baelor wears it in the trial |
| Wrath | Ser Steffon Fossoway's destrier | Yes (mentioned) | Steffon |
| The septon's crystal | Raised to call the throng to prayer before the trial | Yes | Ashford's septon |

## Food & Drink
| Meal/Occasion | Food Items Described | Drink | Who Is Eating/Drinking | Where | Preparation/Presentation Notes |

| Dunk's inn supper | Lamb roasted with a crust of herbs; duck cooked with cherries and lemons ("not near as greasy as most"); buttered pease; oaten bread "still hot from her oven" | Ale, nut-brown and "thick on the tongue"; four tankards | Dunk | The roadside inn | Ducks shot by the innkeep's son; "good lamb"; Dunk paid a silver stag |
| Lords' fare (referenced) | Capons and suckling pigs | — | The lords and great knights | Ashford Meadow pavilions | Contrasted with Dunk's "hard, stringy piece of salt beef" |

| Camp fish | A fish Egg caught and cooked (still raw inside, bones not removed) | — | Dunk and Egg | The green pool camp | "Tasted a world better than hard salt beef" |
| Meadow snacks | Sausages "made with pork," sizzling over a smoky fire | A horn of ale (and half a horn for Egg) | Dunk and Egg | Merchants' row | Bought with coppers |
| Day-one breakfast | Goose eggs, fried bread, bacon | — | Dunk and Egg | The camp | Dunk had no appetite; belly "hard as a rock" |
| Fossoway pavilion hospitality | A goat basted with honey and herbs over a cookfire | Arbor vintage wine, "fine and fruity" | Dunk and Raymun | Ser Steffon's gold pavilion | Servants basting the goat; brazier warming the air |
| Dunk's cell meal | Hot bread and honey; a bowl of pease porridge; a skewer of roast onions and well-charred goat "flavored with some lordly spice he'd never tasted before" | — | Dunk (served by Egg) | The tower cell | No knife provided; "Grease ran down his chin" |
| Baelor's solar | — | Sweet Dornish red wine | Dunk (poured by Egg/Aegon) | Lord Ashford's solar | Served in a silver goblet |

## Hospitality & Guest Right

|-------|------|------|----------|---------|
| Supper at the inn | Paid lodging-house meal | The innkeep | Dunk | Dunk declines a room to save coin; pays with a silver stag, gets coppers back |
| Wine and goat at the Fossoway pavilion | Pavilion hospitality | Ser Steffon Fossoway (hosted by Raymun) | Dunk | Arbor vintage and roast goat offered; Dunk uneasy "accepting hospitality he could not return" |
| Wine in Lord Ashford's solar | Princely hospitality | Prince Baelor | Dunk | Sweet Dornish red poured by Egg/Aegon; Baelor delivers the trial choice here |
| Cell meal | Provided to a prisoner | (Lord Ashford / Baelor's order, served by Egg) | Dunk | Hot food brought to the tower cell; no knife "Did they think I'd stab you, boy?" |

## Events & Actions
1. **Dunk buries Ser Arlan of Pennytree** — Dunk digs a grave on a sunset-facing hill and buries his master, who died of a chill after the spring rains; he speaks awkward words in place of prayers.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Ser Arlan's body
   - Location: The low hill
   - Outcome: Burial complete

2. **Dunk takes Ser Arlan's arms and claims knighthood** — He dons the sword belt on a rope, takes the longsword, shield, mail, and three horses, and resolves to ride to Ashford as a knight.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instrument: Ser Arlan's longsword and shield
   - Outcome: Dunk sets out as "Ser Duncan the Tall"

3. **Dunk meets the stableboy Egg at the inn** — He hires the bald boy to tend his horses; later finds Egg mounted on Thunder in Arlan's armor and rebukes him as a thief; refuses to take him to Ashford.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: The inn / stables
   - Outcome: Dunk rides off alone; Egg leaves the dropped copper in the dirt

4. **Daeron warns Dunk of his dream** — The drunk lordling tells Dunk "I dreamed of you" and to "stay well away."
   - Agent: Daeron (Prince Daeron, in disguise as a common lordling)
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: The inn common room
   - Outcome: Dunk puzzled

5. **Egg follows to Ashford and becomes Dunk's squire** — Egg rides in a lamb-cart to the meadow, cooks for Dunk, and persuades him to take him on as squire; Dunk chooses the alias-name "Ser Duncan the Tall."
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: The green pool camp
   - Outcome: Egg sworn as squire "for the tourney"

6. **Dunk meets Aerion at the castle stables** — Aerion mistakes Dunk for a stableboy and demands wine and a wench; Dunk demurs.
   - Agent: Aerion
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: Ashford Castle stables
   - Outcome: Aerion dismisses Dunk; "Knighthood has fallen on sad days"

7. **Dunk sells Sweetfoot to Henly** — After much haggling, the palfrey sells for 750 silver stags (plus a dispute over the saddle), paid partly in gold dragons.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Henly
   - Instrument: Sweetfoot
   - Location: Ashford town stable
   - Outcome: Dunk raises coin for armor; tips coppers for Sweetfoot's oats and apple

8. **Dunk commissions armor from Steely Pate** — He bargains for a plain steel suit; Pate takes his old armor as trade, agreeing to 600 stags with two silver down.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Steely Pate
   - Location: Merchants' row
   - Outcome: Armor promised on the morrow

9. **Plummer and the lords examine Dunk's claim** — The steward doubts Dunk's knighthood; Baelor vouches that he recalls Ser Arlan and quizzes Dunk on the Grey Lion's name ("Ser Damon Lannister"); Dunk is permitted to enter the lists.
   - Agent: Baelor
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: Ashford Castle hall
   - Witness: Maekar, Lord Ashford
   - Outcome: Entry granted; Baelor tells Dunk he must adopt a new sigil

10. **Tanselle repaints Dunk's shield** — Dunk commissions a new device: a green elm on a sunset field with a shooting star.
    - Agent: Tanselle
    - Patient: Dunk's shield
    - Location: Merchants' row
    - Outcome: Shield delivered (via Pate) the night before the trial

11. **Day-one jousting (the champions defend)** — The five champions defend the fair maid; many bouts: Leo Tyrell unhorses/helms Damon Lannister; Humfrey Hardyng beats Lord Tully; Tybolt Lannister beats Androw Ashford; the Laughing Storm beats Robert Ashford; Valarr unhorses Abelar Hightower and later beats Lord Gawen Swann; Leo Tyrell beats Pearse Caron and Robyn Rhysling; Hardyng downs Joseth Mallister; Tybolt wins fighting with shield alone after Jon Penrose unhorses him.
    - Agent: The champions (Leo Tyrell, Humfrey Hardyng, Tybolt Lannister, Lyonel Baratheon, Valarr)
    - Patient: Various challengers
    - Location: Ashford Meadow lists
    - Outcome: Tybolt Lannister and the Laughing Storm join the champions; both Ashford sons fall

12. **Aerion kills Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse** — Aerion drives his lance low into Hardyng's stallion's breast; the horse falls, crushing Hardyng's leg; Aerion advances with sword and must be restrained.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Ser Humfrey Hardyng's stallion (and Hardyng)
    - Instrument: War lance
    - Location: Ashford Meadow lists
    - Witness: Dunk, Egg
    - Outcome: Horse dead; Hardyng's leg broken in two places; Ashford awards Hardyng Aerion's courser

13. **Aerion assaults the puppeteers and breaks Tanselle's finger** — His men smash and burn the puppets; Aerion twists Tanselle's arm and snaps one of her fingers.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Tanselle
    - Location: Merchants' row
    - Witness: Egg, a crowd of watchers
    - Outcome: Tanselle maimed; Egg runs for Dunk

14. **Dunk beats Aerion** — Dunk grabs the prince, knocks him down, and kicks him in the belly and mouth, loosening a tooth, before Aerion's men pin him.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Aerion
    - Location: Merchants' row
    - Witness: Egg, Raymun and Steffon Fossoway (who arrive armed)
    - Outcome: Dunk pinned; Aerion orders his teeth broken

15. **Egg reveals himself as a prince** — Egg commands Aerion's men to release Dunk; they obey; Egg tells Aerion "I cut [my hair] off, brother... I didn't want to look like you."
    - Agent: Egg / Aegon
    - Patient: Aerion's men-at-arms (Wate, Yorkel)
    - Location: Merchants' row
    - Outcome: Dunk freed; identity reveal

16. **Dunk is imprisoned** — Lord Ashford's soldiers scoop up Dunk and the others; Dunk is confined to a tower cell.
    - Agent: Lord Ashford (his soldiers)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Ashford Castle tower cell
    - Outcome: Dunk held overnight and through day two

17. **Baelor offers Dunk the choice of trial by combat** — In the solar, Baelor explains Dunk faces loss of a hand and a foot, and reminds him he may demand trial by combat.
    - Agent: Baelor
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: The solar
    - Outcome: Dunk's fate hinges on combat

18. **Aerion demands a trial of seven** — At the judgment, Aerion invokes his right to a trial of seven so both he and Daeron may face Dunk; seven champions must fight on each side.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Instigator: (Aerion's claim, seconded over Maekar's objection)
    - Location: Ashford Castle hall
    - Outcome: Dunk must find six more knights

19. **Dunk recruits his champions; Steffon betrays him** — Ser Steffon pledges aid then reveals he fights for the accusers ("I am the seventh... but for the other side"); Raymun, Lyonel Baratheon, the two Humfreys, Robyn Rhysling, and (secretly) Baelor take Dunk's side; Raymun is knighted by Lyonel and adopts a green apple.
    - Agent: Dunk (and Egg, who recruits Lyonel and Rhysling)
    - Patient: Various knights
    - Location: The Fossoway pavilion / paddock
    - Outcome: Six champions, then Baelor as the seventh; Steffon joins Aerion

20. **Baelor secretly joins Dunk's side** — When no knight answers Dunk's plea, a black knight in Valarr's armor rides out; he lifts his visor to reveal Prince Baelor.
    - Agent: Baelor
    - Patient: Dunk (champion of)
    - Instrument: Valarr's borrowed armor
    - Location: The lists
    - Outcome: Dunk's seventh found; "This man protected the weak, as every true knight must"

21. **The Trial of Seven** — Seven defenders against seven accusers charge with tourney lances (Baelor's stratagem) versus war lances. Donnel of Duskendale kills Humfrey Beesbury; Aerion drives a lance through Dunk's side; Baelor unhorses Kingsguard and fights Maekar; Dunk is unhorsed twice and head-struck by Aerion's morningstar.
    - Agent: The seven defenders and seven accusers
    - Patient: Each other
    - Instrument: Tourney lances, war lances, morningstar, mace, swords
    - Location: Ashford Meadow (muddy field)
    - Outcome: See sub-events below

22. **Dunk defeats Aerion and forces his recantation** — Dunk rolls free, drags Aerion down, batters his helm with a shield, wrenches up his visor, and makes him yield; later drags him before Lord Ashford to withdraw the accusation.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Aerion
    - Instrument: Aerion's own oak-and-iron shield
    - Location: The trial field
    - Outcome: "I withdraw my accusation"; trial won for Dunk

23. **Maekar's mace kills Baelor** — Fighting his brother, Maekar lands a mace blow to the back of Baelor's borrowed helm; the blow crushes Baelor's skull. He stands, speaks, then collapses when the helm is removed.
    - Agent: Maekar
    - Patient: Baelor
    - Instrument: Spiked mace
    - Location: The trial field
    - Witness: Dunk, Raymun, Steely Pate
    - Outcome: Baelor's death (delayed; he dies as the helm comes off)

24. **Baelor's funeral** — As Targaryens, Baelor is given to the fire in Ashford Castle's yard, in a black velvet tunic with a scarlet dragon and a golden open-visored helm.
    - Agent: House Targaryen (Daeron II's wishes prevail)
    - Patient: Baelor's body
    - Location: Ashford Castle yard
    - Outcome: Cremation

25. **Valarr dismisses Dunk** — Standing vigil, Valarr asks "Why would the gods take him, and leave you?" and bids Dunk begone.
    - Agent: Valarr
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Ashford Castle
    - Outcome: Dunk leaves wordless

26. **Maekar banishes Aerion and offers Egg to Dunk** — Maekar sends Aerion to Lys, then offers Dunk a place in his household at Summerhall with Egg as squire; Dunk accepts Egg but insists on taking him on the road instead.
    - Agent: Maekar
    - Patient: Aerion (banished); Egg (given as squire)
    - Location: The green pool camp
    - Outcome: Maekar walks off silent, then sends Egg the next morning

27. **Dunk and Egg set out for Dorne** — Egg arrives at sunrise to serve Dunk; they prepare to ride over the Red Mountains.
    - Agent: Dunk and Egg
    - Location: The green pool camp
    - Outcome: The pair depart together

## Spatial Layout & Movement
| Phase | Who | Position / Movement | Relative To | Notes |
|-------|-----|---------------------|-------------|-------|
| Departure | Dunk | Rides from the burial hill toward Ashford, leading three horses | The low hill → the road | Stops at the inn en route |
| Arrival | Dunk | Reins up at the edge of Ashford Meadow; turns away to make camp in the trees | Edge of the meadow → the green pool | Half a mile from town and castle |

input.content:
# THK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** THK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (with Ashford Castle and Ashford town)
- **location_secondary:** A low hill on the road (Ser Arlan's grave); an inn beside a stream a day's ride from Ashford; Dunk's camp by the green pool beneath the elm; a tower cell in Ashford Castle
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; Dunk rides to Ashford, sells a horse, buys armor, enters the lists; covers the first and second days of the tourney, the puppet-show assault, Dunk's arrest, the trial of seven, Baelor's death and funeral, and the aftermath where Dunk takes Egg as squire.
- **time_markers:** spring (spring rains, spring grass, spring air); the old man's dying: rains "the morrow," wet gusty winds "the one after," a chill "the next," "By the fourth day the old man was too weak to ride," "now he was gone," "Only a few days past he had been singing"; "A day's ride" from the inn to Ashford; "It was full dark" when Dunk left the inn; "the shadows of the afternoon were growing long" arriving at the meadow; first day of tourney "dawned bright and clear"; second day "overcast"; "The night was half gone" before the trial; "the first wan light"/"dawn"; Ser Arlan "closer to sixty than to fifty"; Dunk "sixteen or seventeen years"; Egg "no more than eight or nine," "eight-year-old"; the fair maid's "thirteenth nameday"; Daeron "has only eighteen years"; the last dragon seen "the year before it died"; Otho Bracken slew Quentyn Blackwood "three years past"; Arlan unhorsed Damon Lannister "sixteen years ago" at King's Landing; Storm's End hastilude "nine years past"; Robyn Rhysling lost his eye "not five years earlier"; "more than a hundred years" since the last trial of seven; Ser Arlan had not ridden a tilt "many years before"; Ser Humfrey Hardyng died "only last night."
- **chapter_summary:** After burying his master Ser Arlan of Pennytree, the hedge knight Dunk takes the old man's arms and rides to the tourney at Ashford Meadow, taking on a bald orphan stableboy named Egg as his squire. Dunk sells a horse to buy new armor and, with Prince Baelor's intervention, is permitted to enter the lists. When Dunk beats Prince Aerion Brightflame for assaulting the puppeteer Tanselle, he is arrested for striking the blood of the dragon and faces a trial of seven. Six knights and ultimately Prince Baelor Breakspear himself take Dunk's side against Aerion, Daeron, Maekar, three Kingsguard, and the turncoat Ser Steffon Fossoway; Dunk forces Aerion to yield, but Baelor dies of a head wound dealt by his brother Maekar's mace. Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen, and his father Maekar gives him to Dunk as squire, the two riding off toward Dorne.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Spring rains that softened the ground; wet gusty winds; a chill; rain threatening as Dunk dug the grave; first tourney day "bright and clear"; second day "overcast, with a gusty wind blowing from the west"; a soft rain falling "light as dew" the night before the trial; the trial morning rain tapering off at first light leaving fog; "Tendrils of fog were writhing along the ground like pale white snakes"; "pale blowing mist"/"river mist" on the field; "bleak grey sky."
- **Season indicators:** Spring throughout — "spring rains," "spring grass," "the spring air," "at least he had lived to see another spring"; lore that "the summers have been shorter since the last dragon died, and the winters longer and crueler."
- **Time of day:** Sunset (Arlan loved to watch the sunset; Dunk buries him on the western slope); full dark leaving the inn; moonlit, cloudless, star-speckled night riding to the meadow; "It was full dark"; a falling star at night; cock crowing at dawn; torchlit evenings on merchants' row; dawn of the trial.
- **Lighting:** Warm yellow light spilling from the inn windows; torchlight along merchant's row ("a hundred torches"); firelight of campfires; an oil lamp and an iron lantern; a brazier of coals; beeswax candle; "colored rings haloed the scant few pavilions where fires still burned."
- **Sounds:** The old song Arlan sang; a robin heard at the dubbing; a horse whickering; cock crowing; distant tourney music ("pipes and drums"); the ring of hammers/carpenters; trumpets and herald's horns; the crash of lances, ring of steel; the crowd's roar "like the crash of distant waves"; the crack of Tanselle's finger breaking; the screams of Ser Humfrey's dying horse "almost human"; a man screaming "high and terrible"; "the faint thrum of the dragonfly's wings."
- **Smells:** The smell of rain in the air; woodsmoke and bacon; sausages sizzling over a smoky fire; roasting goat basted with honey and herbs.
- **Notable sensory details:** A dragonfly skimming the green pool (recurs at the close); the field churned to "grey-brown mud and torn grass" after rain; the heat and packed mud inside Dunk's greathelm during the trial; the "blue-grey and pulpy" matter and bone visible in Baelor's crushed skull; a green falling star streaking the black sky.

## Characters Present

## Events & Actions
1. **Dunk buries Ser Arlan of Pennytree** — He digs a grave on a western slope and lays the old man in it, stripped of armor and sword, saying clumsy words in place of prayers.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Ser Arlan (corpse)
   - Location: The low hill / western slope
   - Outcome: Burial
2. **Dunk takes up Ser Arlan's sword and shield and rides for Ashford** — He claims the old man's arms, inventories his goods, and resolves to compete at the tourney.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instrument: Ser Arlan's longsword and shield
   - Outcome: Departure for Ashford
3. **Dunk meets the bald stableboy at the inn** — A naked boy from the stream agrees (grudgingly) to tend his horses; Dunk dines while the drunk lordling warns him to "stay away."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: The inn
   - Outcome: First encounter
4. **Egg is caught riding Thunder in Arlan's armor** — Dunk finds the boy mounted in the warhorse's gear; the boy begs to be taken as a squire; Dunk refuses and leaves alone.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Thunder
   - Location: The inn stables
   - Witness: Dunk
   - Outcome: Dunk leaves; the boy's copper lies in the dirt
5. **Egg follows to the camp and Dunk takes him as squire** — Egg, having ridden in a cart, is found at the camp; he reveals his name and Dunk relents, taking him on for the tourney; Dunk invents the name "Ser Duncan the Tall."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: The green pool camp
   - Outcome: Egg becomes Dunk's squire
6. **Dunk meets Aerion at the castle stables** — Aerion mistakes Dunk for a stableboy and demands wine and a wench; Dunk demurs.
   - Agent: Aerion
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: Ashford Castle stables
   - Outcome: Aerion dismisses Dunk
7. **Dunk sells Sweetfoot to Henly** — After being turned away by Ashford's master of horse, Dunk haggles the town stableman down to 750 silver stags for the palfrey.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Sweetfoot (sold)
   - Location: Ashford town
   - Outcome: Dunk raises coin for armor
8. **Dunk commissions armor from Steely Pate** — He pays two silvers down on a plain steel suit, promising the balance on the morrow.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Steely Pate (contracted)

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Grief over Ser Arlan's death; loneliness ("he only has me"); anxious hope about competing; awe and intimidation before lords and princes; shame at his low birth and clumsiness with words ("Dunk the lunk, thick as a castle wall"); terror facing the trial and possible mutilation; despair when no knight will join him; guilt and grief after Baelor dies for his sake.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Proving himself a true knight rather than a hedge knight — winning "one victory" to earn a place in noble company; later, surviving the trial of seven; finally, what he owes Baelor and what to do with Egg.
- **Key decisions made:** To bury Ser Arlan and take his arms; to take Egg as squire; to sell Sweetfoot and buy armor; to enter the lists under the name "Ser Duncan the Tall"; to defend Tanselle by attacking Aerion; to choose trial by combat over mutilation; to swear himself Baelor's man; to take Aegon as squire on the road rather than at Summerhall, heading for Dorne.
- **Self-deception flags:** Tells himself the stableboy will scoop up the spurned copper "as soon as I am gone"; insists "one victory is all I need"; rationalizes that taking Egg from the inn "would be no kindness"; convinces himself the smallfolk's goodwill and the painted "death" shield are ill omens, then accepts Pate's reframing; repeatedly braces against the old man's verdict that he is "thick as a castle wall."

| Dunk | MOURNS | Prince Baelor | "He could not see the wound without thinking of Baelor." |
| Ser Roland Crakehall | MEMBER_OF | the Kingsguard | "I am Ser Roland Crakehall, and this is my Sworn Brother, Ser Donnel of Duskendale." |
| Ser Donnel of Duskendale | MEMBER_OF | the Kingsguard | "my Sworn Brother, Ser Donnel of Duskendale." |
| Ser Willem Wylde | MEMBER_OF | the Kingsguard | "Ser Roland Crakehall, Ser Donnel of Duskendale, and Ser Willem Wylde." |
| Aerion | DUELS | Dunk | The accuser and accused fight in the trial of seven; "only Aerion matters." |
| Prince Maekar | SWORN_TO (current) | King Daeron II | Maekar acts under the king's law to "redeem Aerion's honor"; the king is his father and liege |

## Unanswered Questions
| Question | Raised By | Context |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| Who are Dunk's true parents, and what is his real lineage? | Narration / Dunk | "He had never known his father or mother"; an orphan of Flea Bottom with no house |
| What was the meaning of Baelor's death — a prince dying so a hedge knight might live? | Dunk and Maekar | "The world made no sense when a great prince died so a hedge knight might live"; Maekar: "Perhaps none of this had any meaning." |
| What does Daeron's dream of Dunk and a dead dragon mean? | Daeron / Dunk | "I dreamed of you and a dead dragon... you were alive and the dragon was dead." |
| What new sigil and house-name should Dunk take, having no right to the winged chalice? | Prince Baelor / Dunk | "You must needs find a new device, ser, a sigil of your own." |
| Will Dunk ever have need of the foot a prince's life bought him? | Dunk | "mighten it be that some morrow will come when I'll have need of that foot? When the realm will need that foot, even more than a prince's life?" |
| Will Maekar's whispered guilt ever leave him? | Prince Maekar | "I will hear the whispers till the day I die"; "Is that a mercy or a curse?" |
| Will Aerion ever return from Lys? | Dunk | "hoped he never came back" |

input.content:
# THK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** THK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (with Ashford Castle and Ashford town)
- **location_secondary:** A low hill on the road (Ser Arlan's grave); an inn beside a stream a day's ride from Ashford; Dunk's camp by the green pool beneath the elm; a tower cell in Ashford Castle
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; Dunk rides to Ashford, sells a horse, buys armor, enters the lists; covers the first and second days of the tourney, the puppet-show assault, Dunk's arrest, the trial of seven, Baelor's death and funeral, and the aftermath where Dunk takes Egg as squire.
- **time_markers:** spring (spring rains, spring grass, spring air); the old man's dying: rains "the morrow," wet gusty winds "the one after," a chill "the next," "By the fourth day the old man was too weak to ride," "now he was gone," "Only a few days past he had been singing"; "A day's ride" from the inn to Ashford; "It was full dark" when Dunk left the inn; "the shadows of the afternoon were growing long" arriving at the meadow; first day of tourney "dawned bright and clear"; second day "overcast"; "The night was half gone" before the trial; "the first wan light"/"dawn"; Ser Arlan "closer to sixty than to fifty"; Dunk "sixteen or seventeen years"; Egg "no more than eight or nine," "eight-year-old"; the fair maid's "thirteenth nameday"; Daeron "has only eighteen years"; the last dragon seen "the year before it died"; Otho Bracken slew Quentyn Blackwood "three years past"; Arlan unhorsed Damon Lannister "sixteen years ago" at King's Landing; Storm's End hastilude "nine years past"; Robyn Rhysling lost his eye "not five years earlier"; "more than a hundred years" since the last trial of seven; Ser Arlan had not ridden a tilt "many years before"; Ser Humfrey Hardyng died "only last night."
- **chapter_summary:** After burying his master Ser Arlan of Pennytree, the hedge knight Dunk takes the old man's arms and rides to the tourney at Ashford Meadow, taking on a bald orphan stableboy named Egg as his squire. Dunk sells a horse to buy new armor and, with Prince Baelor's intervention, is permitted to enter the lists. When Dunk beats Prince Aerion Brightflame for assaulting the puppeteer Tanselle, he is arrested for striking the blood of the dragon and faces a trial of seven. Six knights and ultimately Prince Baelor Breakspear himself take Dunk's side against Aerion, Daeron, Maekar, three Kingsguard, and the turncoat Ser Steffon Fossoway; Dunk forces Aerion to yield, but Baelor dies of a head wound dealt by his brother Maekar's mace. Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen, and his father Maekar gives him to Dunk as squire, the two riding off toward Dorne.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Spring rains that softened the ground; wet gusty winds; a chill; rain threatening as Dunk dug the grave; first tourney day "bright and clear"; second day "overcast, with a gusty wind blowing from the west"; a soft rain falling "light as dew" the night before the trial; the trial morning rain tapering off at first light leaving fog; "Tendrils of fog were writhing along the ground like pale white snakes"; "pale blowing mist"/"river mist" on the field; "bleak grey sky."
- **Season indicators:** Spring throughout — "spring rains," "spring grass," "the spring air," "at least he had lived to see another spring"; lore that "the summers have been shorter since the last dragon died, and the winters longer and crueler."
- **Time of day:** Sunset (Arlan loved to watch the sunset; Dunk buries him on the western slope); full dark leaving the inn; moonlit, cloudless, star-speckled night riding to the meadow; "It was full dark"; a falling star at night; cock crowing at dawn; torchlit evenings on merchants' row; dawn of the trial.
- **Lighting:** Warm yellow light spilling from the inn windows; torchlight along merchant's row ("a hundred torches"); firelight of campfires; an oil lamp and an iron lantern; a brazier of coals; beeswax candle; "colored rings haloed the scant few pavilions where fires still burned."
- **Sounds:** The old song Arlan sang; a robin heard at the dubbing; a horse whickering; cock crowing; distant tourney music ("pipes and drums"); the ring of hammers/carpenters; trumpets and herald's horns; the crash of lances, ring of steel; the crowd's roar "like the crash of distant waves"; the crack of Tanselle's finger breaking; the screams of Ser Humfrey's dying horse "almost human"; a man screaming "high and terrible"; "the faint thrum of the dragonfly's wings."
- **Smells:** The smell of rain in the air; woodsmoke and bacon; sausages sizzling over a smoky fire; roasting goat basted with honey and herbs.
- **Notable sensory details:** A dragonfly skimming the green pool (recurs at the close); the field churned to "grey-brown mud and torn grass" after rain; the heat and packed mud inside Dunk's greathelm during the trial; the "blue-grey and pulpy" matter and bone visible in Baelor's crushed skull; a green falling star streaking the black sky.

## Characters Present
| Character | Role in Chapter | First Appearance? | Notes |
|-----------|----------------|-------------------|-------|
| Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall) | POV; hedge knight, newly knighted | Yes | Real name revealed as Duncan; born in Flea Bottom; squired to Ser Arlan |
| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | Dunk's late master; buried in opening scene | Yes | Dead at start; present only as corpse, then recalled throughout |
| Egg (Prince Aegon Targaryen) | Stableboy who becomes Dunk's squire | Yes | Bald; revealed in-text to be Aegon, son of Maekar |
| The innkeep (whey-faced woman) | Serves Dunk at the inn | Yes | Stout, short; has a son and a daughter; son shot the ducks |
| Daeron Targaryen (the drunk lordling) | Passed-out lordling at the inn; later an accuser | Yes | Eldest son of Maekar; "Daeron the Drunken"; has true dreams |
| Steely Pate | Armorer; forges Dunk's armor and new shield rim | Yes | Five feet tall, black beard, huge hands |
| Tanselle (Too-Tall) | Dornish puppeteer who paints Dunk's shield | Yes | Tall, olive-skinned, black-haired; her finger is broken by Aerion |
| The fat Dornishwoman | Puppeteer who works one set of puppets | Yes | Works Florian/the puppet knight |
| Plummer | Steward of Ashford; master of the games | Yes | Thinning grey hair, narrow pinched face |
| Guards captain of Ashford | Escorts Dunk to Plummer | Yes | Short, bearded |
| Lord Ashford's master of horse | Declines to buy Sweetfoot | Yes | Red-faced, brusque, orange livery |
| Aerion Brightflame | Antagonist prince; accuser | Yes | Son of Maekar; silver-gold hair, violet eyes; sent to Lys at the end |
| Ser Roland Crakehall | Kingsguard | Yes | Bigger of two white knights met in yard |
| Ser Donnel of Duskendale | Kingsguard | Yes | Red of hair and beard; kills Beesbury in the trial |
| Henly | Stableman of Ashford town; buys Sweetfoot | Yes | Haggles Dunk down to 750 silver stags |
| Ser Steffon Fossoway | Knight; pledges to Dunk then betrays him | Yes | Senior branch of House Fossoway; joins the accusers |
| Raymun Fossoway | Squire; befriends Dunk; knighted in the trial | Yes | Cousin to Steffon; changes his apple to green |
| Ser Manfred Dondarrion | Knight who refuses to vouch for Dunk | Yes | Thin, sour; red-gold hair |
| Prince Baelor Breakspear | Hand of the King; takes Dunk's side; dies | Yes | Heir apparent; dark hair; twice-broken nose |
| Prince Maekar | Baelor's brother; an accuser; kills Baelor | Yes | Prince of Summerhall; silvery beard, pox-scarred |
| Lord Ashford | Host of the tourney; one of the judges | Yes | Big bald man, round red face |
| Lord Leo Tyrell (Leo Longthorn) | Champion; one of the judges | Yes | Lord of Highgarden; silver-haired; peerless jouster |
| Ser Lyonel Baratheon (the Laughing Storm) | Champion; fights for Dunk | Yes | Crowned stag; antlered helm; knights Raymun |
| Ser Humfrey Hardyng | Champion; fights for Dunk; later dies of wounds | Yes | Red-and-white diamonds; leg broken by Aerion |
| Ser Humfrey Beesbury | Knight; fights for Dunk; killed in trial | Yes | Yellow-and-black stripes, three beehives |
| Ser Robyn Rhysling | One-eyed knight; fights for Dunk | Yes | Salt-and-pepper beard; lost eye to a lance splinter |
| Valarr (the Young Prince) | Champion; Baelor's son; stands vigil at the bier | Yes | Black armor, three-headed dragon; bright streak in brown hair |
| Ser Damon Lannister (the Grey Lion) | Champion challenger; Lord of Casterly Rock | Yes | Unhorsed by Arlan years past; helm struck off by Leo Tyrell |
| Ser Tybolt Lannister | Challenger; Damon's heir | Yes | Golden-haired; unhorsed by Jon Penrose but wins on foot |
| Ser Abelar Hightower | Challenger; unhorsed by Valarr | Yes | Silver-and-smoke; stone watchtower crowned with fire |
| Lord Tully of Riverrun | Challenger; beaten by Humfrey Hardyng | Yes | Blue-and-red striped cloak, silver trout clasps |
| Ser Joseth Mallister | Challenger; carried off unconscious | Yes | Winged helm; silver eagle on indigo |
| Ser Pearse Caron (Lord of the Marches) | Challenger; harper-knight | Yes | Silver harp; nightingales; loses to Leo Tyrell |
| Lord Gawen Swann | Challenger; yields to Valarr | Yes | Lord of Stonehelm; black-and-white swans |
| Ser Otho Bracken (the Brute of Bracken) | Tourney knight; refuses to join Dunk | Yes | Red stallion on brown; slew Quentyn Blackwood |
| Androw Ashford | Lord Ashford's son; a champion | Yes | Orange sun-and-chevron; defeated by Tybolt Lannister |
| Ser Robert Ashford | Lord Ashford's son; a champion | Yes | Defeated by Lyonel Baratheon after long fight |
| Ser Jon Penrose | Challenger; unhorses Tybolt Lannister | Yes | Mentioned in jousting |
| Ser Grance | Knight Steffon calls to spar | Yes | Brief appearance in training yard |
| The fair maid / Lady Ashford | Queen of Love and Beauty | Yes | Short, yellow hair, round pink face; thirteenth nameday |
| Wate | Aerion's man-at-arms | Yes | Ordered to break Dunk's teeth |
| Yorkel | Aerion's man-at-arms | Yes | Named by Egg |
| Lord Ashford's septon | Calls the throng to prayer before the trial | Yes | Raises his crystal |
| Ser Willem Wylde | Kingsguard; accuser | Yes | Carried from field insensate |
| The Tyroshi armorer | Sells ornate helms at the meadow | Yes | Forked blue beard |

## Character Appearances
| Character | Hair | Eyes | Build/Height | Scars/Marks | Clothing/Armor | Weapons Worn | Distinguishing Features | Age (if stated) |
|-----------|------|------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------|
| Dunk | shaggy | — | hugely tall, "closer to seven feet than to six," big-boned, shambling, just begun to fill out; strong as an aurochs | gains a "deep puckered scar between his left arm and his nipple" from the lance wound | roughspun tunic and breeches; rope/hempen sword belt; later a full suit of plain steel from Steely Pate | longsword, dagger, war lance | thick as a castle wall (self-described); never been with a woman | sixteen or seventeen (no one certain) |
| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | — | — | small, slim; "stripped of hauberk, helm, and sword belt, he seemed to weigh no more than a bag of leaves" | — | hauberk, helm, sword belt (removed for burial) | longsword (black stone pommel), war lance | gentle, generous in praise; could not read or write but knew heraldry | "closer to sixty than to fifty" |

## Events & Actions
1. **Dunk buries Ser Arlan of Pennytree** — He digs a grave on a western slope and lays the old man in it, stripped of armor and sword, saying clumsy words in place of prayers.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Ser Arlan (corpse)
   - Location: The low hill / western slope
   - Outcome: Burial
2. **Dunk takes up Ser Arlan's sword and shield and rides for Ashford** — He claims the old man's arms, inventories his goods, and resolves to compete at the tourney.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instrument: Ser Arlan's longsword and shield
   - Outcome: Departure for Ashford
3. **Dunk meets the bald stableboy at the inn** — A naked boy from the stream agrees (grudgingly) to tend his horses; Dunk dines while the drunk lordling warns him to "stay away."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: The inn
   - Outcome: First encounter
4. **Egg is caught riding Thunder in Arlan's armor** — Dunk finds the boy mounted in the warhorse's gear; the boy begs to be taken as a squire; Dunk refuses and leaves alone.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Thunder
   - Location: The inn stables
   - Witness: Dunk
   - Outcome: Dunk leaves; the boy's copper lies in the dirt
5. **Egg follows to the camp and Dunk takes him as squire** — Egg, having ridden in a cart, is found at the camp; he reveals his name and Dunk relents, taking him on for the tourney; Dunk invents the name "Ser Duncan the Tall."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: The green pool camp
   - Outcome: Egg becomes Dunk's squire
6. **Dunk meets Aerion at the castle stables** — Aerion mistakes Dunk for a stableboy and demands wine and a wench; Dunk demurs.
   - Agent: Aerion
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: Ashford Castle stables
   - Outcome: Aerion dismisses Dunk
7. **Dunk sells Sweetfoot to Henly** — After being turned away by Ashford's master of horse, Dunk haggles the town stableman down to 750 silver stags for the palfrey.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Sweetfoot (sold)
   - Location: Ashford town
   - Outcome: Dunk raises coin for armor
8. **Dunk commissions armor from Steely Pate** — He pays two silvers down on a plain steel suit, promising the balance on the morrow.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Steely Pate (contracted)
   - Location: Merchants' row
   - Outcome: Armor reserved for a day
9. **Dunk seeks entry to the lists; Baelor vouches for him** — Plummer doubts him; in the Great Hall Dunk correctly names the Grey Lion (Damon Lannister), and Prince Baelor, recalling Ser Arlan, permits his entry and rules he must adopt a new sigil.
   - Agent: Prince Baelor
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Location: Ashford Castle Great Hall
   - Witness: Maekar, Lord Ashford, Plummer
   - Outcome: Dunk allowed to enter the lists
10. **Tanselle agrees to paint Dunk's shield** — Dunk asks the Dornish puppet girl to paint over the chalice; he and Egg devise a sunset field, an elm tree, and a shooting star.
    - Agent: Tanselle
    - Patient: Dunk's shield
    - Location: The puppet show / fairground
    - Outcome: New sigil designed
11. **First day of jousting** — Champions (the two Ashford sons, Leo Tyrell, Humfrey Hardyng, Valarr) defend against challengers; Valarr unhorses Abelar Hightower; Leo Tyrell strikes off Damon Lannister's helm; Humfrey Hardyng beats Lord Tully; Lyonel Baratheon beats Robert Ashford; Tybolt Lannister beats Androw Ashford; Valarr unhorses Gawen Swann; Leo Tyrell beats Pearse Caron and Robyn Rhysling; Tybolt loses his seat to Jon Penrose but wins on foot; "the Battle of Humfrey" between the two Humfreys.
    - Agent: The competing knights
    - Location: Ashford Meadow lists
    - Witness: Dunk (with Egg on his shoulders)
    - Outcome: Tybolt Lannister and Lyonel Baratheon become champions; both Ashford sons fall
12. **Aerion kills Ser Humfrey Hardyng's horse** — Prince Aerion deliberately lowers his lance and drives it through the breast and neck of Hardyng's stallion, crushing Hardyng's leg.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Hardyng's horse (and Ser Humfrey Hardyng, leg broken)
    - Instrument: Aerion's war lance
    - Location: The lists
    - Witness: Dunk and Egg
    - Outcome: Horse killed; Hardyng maimed; Ashford awards Hardyng Aerion's courser
13. **Aerion assaults Tanselle at the puppet show** — Aerion's men smash and burn the puppets; Aerion twists Tanselle's arm and breaks her finger, calling the slain-dragon puppet treason.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Tanselle
    - Instigator: Aerion (his men-at-arms destroy the puppets)
    - Location: Merchants' row / puppet stall
    - Witness: Egg (who runs for Dunk), then Dunk
    - Outcome: Tanselle's finger broken; the puppets destroyed
14. **Dunk beats Prince Aerion** — Dunk knocks Aerion down, kicks him in the belly and mouth, loosening a tooth, before Aerion's men overwhelm and pin him.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Aerion
    - Location: Merchants' row
    - Witness: Egg, Raymun, Ser Steffon, the crowd
    - Outcome: Aerion's tooth loosened; Dunk seized
15. **Egg reveals his royal authority to stop the men-at-arms** — Egg ("they'll answer to my father... and my uncle") commands Wate and Yorkel to release Dunk; Raymun and Ser Steffon arrive armed.
    - Agent: Egg / Aegon
    - Patient: Aerion's men-at-arms
    - Location: The puppet stall
    - Outcome: Dunk released; identity reveal ("I didn't want to look like you")
16. **Dunk is arrested and confined** — Lord Ashford's soldiers scoop up the party; Dunk is held in a tower cell, his sword, dagger, and silver taken.
    - Agent: Lord Ashford's soldiers
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instigator: Lord Ashford
    - Location: Ashford Castle tower cell
    - Outcome: Dunk imprisoned
17. **Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen** — In the cell, Egg, now in a dragon-emblazoned doublet, confesses he is Aegon, son of Maekar; Daeron shaved his head to hide the Targaryen hair.
    - Agent: Egg / Aegon
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: The tower cell
    - Outcome: Egg's true identity disclosed to Dunk
18. **Baelor offers Dunk trial by combat** — Baelor explains Dunk must be tried for striking the blood of the dragon (penalty: loss of hand and foot), then offers him trial by combat.
    - Agent: Prince Baelor
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Lord Ashford's solar
    - Outcome: Dunk faces trial
19. **Aerion demands a trial of seven** — Before the judges (Baelor, Maekar, Ashford, Leo Tyrell), Aerion invokes the ancient trial of seven so Daeron may also face Dunk; Dunk must find six knights.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instigator: Aerion
    - Location: Ashford Castle hall
    - Outcome: A trial of seven is set
20. **Dunk recruits champions; Daeron warns him** — Via Egg and Raymun, Ser Humfrey Hardyng, Ser Humfrey Beesbury, Ser Robyn Rhysling, and Ser Lyonel Baratheon join; Daeron secretly warns Dunk the Kingsguard will fight for the accusers.
    - Agent: Dunk (and Egg, Raymun)
    - Patient: The recruited knights
    - Location: The Fossoway pavilion and the camp
    - Outcome: Five champions gathered
21. **Ser Steffon Fossoway betrays Dunk** — At the paddock, Steffon dons his mail and reveals he fights for Aerion and the accusers, seeking a lordship; Raymun renounces him.
    - Agent: Ser Steffon Fossoway
    - Patient: Dunk (and Raymun)
    - Location: The challengers' paddock
    - Witness: Dunk, Raymun, the other defenders
    - Outcome: Dunk left one knight short
22. **Ser Lyonel knights Raymun Fossoway** — As Dunk is summoned away, Lyonel Baratheon dubs Raymun, who changes his apple from red to green.
    - Agent: Ser Lyonel Baratheon
    - Patient: Raymun Fossoway
    - Instrument: Lyonel's sword
    - Location: The paddock
    - Outcome: Raymun made a knight
23. **Prince Baelor takes Dunk's side as the seventh** — When no other knight will answer Dunk's plea, Baelor rides out in Valarr's borrowed armor and lifts his visor to fight for him.
    - Agent: Prince Baelor
    - Patient: Dunk (defended)
    - Instrument: Valarr's borrowed armor
    - Location: Ashford Meadow lists
    - Witness: The assembled lords, Maekar, Aerion
    - Outcome: Dunk's side complete at seven
24. **The trial of seven is fought** — The two sides charge with tourney lances; Beesbury is killed by Donnel of Duskendale; Baelor unhorses a Kingsguard and Maekar; Robyn Rhysling unhorses Daeron; Dunk and Aerion are both unhorsed and fight on.
    - Agent: The fourteen combatants
    - Patient: Each other
    - Instrument: Lances, swords, maces, morningstar
    - Location: The lists
    - Outcome: Mass melee; multiple wounded
25. **Aerion wounds Dunk with a lance** — Aerion drives his lance through Dunk's side; Dunk pulls the iron point from his own flesh.
    - Agent: Aerion
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: Aerion's war lance
    - Location: The lists
    - Outcome: Dunk gravely wounded (lasting scar)
26. **Dunk defeats Aerion and forces him to yield** — Knocked down and his helm smashed by the morningstar, Dunk uses Flea Bottom brawling, pins Aerion, batters him with his own shield, and makes him yield, then drags him to Lord Ashford to withdraw the accusation.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Aerion
    - Instrument: Aerion's own shield
    - Location: The lists / before the viewing stand
    - Witness: Lord Ashford, the fair maid, the crowd
    - Outcome: Aerion yields and withdraws his accusation
27. **Maekar kills Baelor with his mace** — Fighting toward his son, Maekar deals Baelor a mace blow to the back of the head; the cracked skull is revealed only when the helm is removed afterward.
    - Agent: Maekar
    - Patient: Baelor
    - Instrument: Maekar's spiked mace
    - Location: The lists
    - Witness: Steely Pate, Raymun, Dunk (at the unhelming)
    - Outcome: Baelor's death
28. **Baelor's funeral** — Baelor is burned in the yard of Ashford Castle per Targaryen custom; Valarr stands vigil and rebukes Dunk ("Why would the gods take him, and leave you?").
    - Agent: House Targaryen / Daeron II's wishes
    - Patient: Baelor (corpse)
    - Location: Ashford Castle yard
    - Witness: Dunk, Valarr
    - Outcome: Cremation
29. **Maekar offers Egg to Dunk as squire** — Maekar reveals Aerion is sent to Lys, debates the meaning of Baelor's death with Dunk, and gives Aegon to Dunk; Dunk insists on taking the boy on the road, not at Summerhall; they ride for Dorne.
    - Agent: Prince Maekar
    - Patient: Dunk (and Aegon)
    - Location: The green pool camp
    - Outcome: Egg becomes Dunk's squire in earnest; they set out for Dorne

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Grief over Ser Arlan's death; loneliness ("he only has me"); anxious hope about competing; awe and intimidation before lords and princes; shame at his low birth and clumsiness with words ("Dunk the lunk, thick as a castle wall"); terror facing the trial and possible mutilation; despair when no knight will join him; guilt and grief after Baelor dies for his sake.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Proving himself a true knight rather than a hedge knight — winning "one victory" to earn a place in noble company; later, surviving the trial of seven; finally, what he owes Baelor and what to do with Egg.
- **Key decisions made:** To bury Ser Arlan and take his arms; to take Egg as squire; to sell Sweetfoot and buy armor; to enter the lists under the name "Ser Duncan the Tall"; to defend Tanselle by attacking Aerion; to choose trial by combat over mutilation; to swear himself Baelor's man; to take Aegon as squire on the road rather than at Summerhall, heading for Dorne.
- **Self-deception flags:** Tells himself the stableboy will scoop up the spurned copper "as soon as I am gone"; insists "one victory is all I need"; rationalizes that taking Egg from the inn "would be no kindness"; convinces himself the smallfolk's goodwill and the painted "death" shield are ill omens, then accepts Pate's reframing; repeatedly braces against the old man's verdict that he is "thick as a castle wall."

## Unanswered Questions
| Question | Raised By | Context |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| Who are Dunk's true parents, and what is his real lineage? | Narration / Dunk | "He had never known his father or mother"; an orphan of Flea Bottom with no house |
| What was the meaning of Baelor's death — a prince dying so a hedge knight might live? | Dunk and Maekar | "The world made no sense when a great prince died so a hedge knight might live"; Maekar: "Perhaps none of this had any meaning." |
| What does Daeron's dream of Dunk and a dead dragon mean? | Daeron / Dunk | "I dreamed of you and a dead dragon... you were alive and the dragon was dead." |
| What new sigil and house-name should Dunk take, having no right to the winged chalice? | Prince Baelor / Dunk | "You must needs find a new device, ser, a sigil of your own." |
| Will Dunk ever have need of the foot a prince's life bought him? | Dunk | "mighten it be that some morrow will come when I'll have need of that foot? When the realm will need that foot, even more than a prince's life?" |
| Will Maekar's whispered guilt ever leave him? | Prince Maekar | "I will hear the whispers till the day I die"; "Is that a mercy or a curse?" |
| Will Aerion ever return from Lys? | Dunk | "hoped he never came back" |

# THK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** THK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (with Ashford Castle and Ashford town)
- **location_secondary:** A low hill on the road (Ser Arlan's grave); an inn beside a stream a day's ride from Ashford; Dunk's camp by the green pool beneath the elm; a tower cell in Ashford Castle
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; Dunk rides to Ashford, sells a horse, buys armor, enters the lists; covers the first and second days of the tourney, the puppet-show assault, Dunk's arrest, the trial of seven, Baelor's death and funeral, and the aftermath where Dunk takes Egg as squire.
- **time_markers:** spring (spring rains, spring grass, spring air); the old man's dying: rains "the morrow," wet gusty winds "the one after," a chill "the next," "By the fourth day the old man was too weak to ride," "now he was gone," "Only a few days past he had been singing"; "A day's ride" from the inn to Ashford; "It was full dark" when Dunk left the inn; "the shadows of the afternoon were growing long" arriving at the meadow; first day of tourney "dawned bright and clear"; second day "overcast"; "The night was half gone" before the trial; "the first wan light"/"dawn"; Ser Arlan "closer to sixty than to fifty"; Dunk "sixteen or seventeen years"; Egg "no more than eight or nine," "eight-year-old"; the fair maid's "thirteenth nameday"; Daeron "has only eighteen years"; the last dragon seen "the year before it died"; Otho Bracken slew Quentyn Blackwood "three years past"; Arlan unhorsed Damon Lannister "sixteen years ago" at King's Landing; Storm's End hastilude "nine years past"; Robyn Rhysling lost his eye "not five years earlier"; "more than a hundred years" since the last trial of seven; Ser Arlan had not ridden a tilt "many years before"; Ser Humfrey Hardyng died "only last night."
- **chapter_summary:** After burying his master Ser Arlan of Pennytree, the hedge knight Dunk takes the old man's arms and rides to the tourney at Ashford Meadow, taking on a bald orphan stableboy named Egg as his squire. Dunk sells a horse to buy new armor and, with Prince Baelor's intervention, is permitted to enter the lists. When Dunk beats Prince Aerion Brightflame for assaulting the puppeteer Tanselle, he is arrested for striking the blood of the dragon and faces a trial of seven. Six knights and ultimately Prince Baelor Breakspear himself take Dunk's side against Aerion, Daeron, Maekar, three Kingsguard, and the turncoat Ser Steffon Fossoway; Dunk forces Aerion to yield, but Baelor dies of a head wound dealt by his brother Maekar's mace. Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen, and his father Maekar gives him to Dunk as squire, the two riding off toward Dorne.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Spring rains that softened the ground; wet gusty winds; a chill; rain threatening as Dunk dug the grave; first tourney day "bright and clear"; second day "overcast, with a gusty wind blowing from the west"; a soft rain falling "light as dew" the night before the trial; the trial morning rain tapering off at first light leaving fog; "Tendrils of fog were writhing along the ground like pale white snakes"; "pale blowing mist"/"river mist" on the field; "bleak grey sky."
- **Season indicators:** Spring throughout — "spring rains," "spring grass," "the spring air," "at least he had lived to see another spring"; lore that "the summers have been shorter since the last dragon died, and the winters longer and crueler."
- **Time of day:** Sunset (Arlan loved to watch the sunset; Dunk buries him on the western slope); full dark leaving the inn; moonlit, cloudless, star-speckled night riding to the meadow; "It was full dark"; a falling star at night; cock crowing at dawn; torchlit evenings on merchants' row; dawn of the trial.
- **Lighting:** Warm yellow light spilling from the inn windows; torchlight along merchant's row ("a hundred torches"); firelight of campfires; an oil lamp and an iron lantern; a brazier of coals; beeswax candle; "colored rings haloed the scant few pavilions where fires still burned."
- **Sounds:** The old song Arlan sang; a robin heard at the dubbing; a horse whickering; cock crowing; distant tourney music ("pipes and drums"); the ring of hammers/carpenters; trumpets and herald's horns; the crash of lances, ring of steel; the crowd's roar "like the crash of distant waves"; the crack of Tanselle's finger breaking; the screams of Ser Humfrey's dying horse "almost human"; a man screaming "high and terrible"; "the faint thrum of the dragonfly's wings."
- **Smells:** The smell of rain in the air; woodsmoke and bacon; sausages sizzling over a smoky fire; roasting goat basted with honey and herbs.
- **Notable sensory details:** A dragonfly skimming the green pool (recurs at the close); the field churned to "grey-brown mud and torn grass" after rain; the heat and packed mud inside Dunk's greathelm during the trial; the "blue-grey and pulpy" matter and bone visible in Baelor's crushed skull; a green falling star streaking the black sky.
